text: More supply chain risks for SMEs. [Knowledge article](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/cybersecurity/actueel.html). [Cybersecurity](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/cybersecurity/veilig/index.html). 29/12/2025 • 2 minutes reading. ABN AMRO Editorial.

metadata.source_title: More supply chain risks for SMEs

text: Digital risks increasingly arise outside the own company.

metadata.source_title: More supply chain risks for SMEs

text: In November, the number of supply chain incidents increased again. Mainly due to vulnerabilities in software and the abuse of zero-day leaks. Attackers target software that is used in thousands of organizations simultaneously.

metadata.source_title: More supply chain risks for SMEs

text: As a result, one weak spot in the chain can immediately have a major impact.

metadata.source_title: More supply chain risks for SMEs

text: Demonstrate NIS-2 conditions. More and more large companies are including NIS2 conditions in their purchasing policy. They require demonstrable cyber hygiene and clear patch management from suppliers. The demand for NIS2-QM certification is therefore increasing noticeably, especially among SMEs that want to meet the requirements of their chain partners. To collaborate with major customers, you must act now.

metadata.source_title: More supply chain risks for SMEs

text:
Cybersecurity step-by-step plan. 1. Gain insight into your software and dependencies. Map out which systems you use, which suppliers belong to them and how quickly vulnerabilities are resolved.
2. Check whether suppliers have demonstrably implemented basic measures. Asking about certification works fastest, but you can also ask about details such as patch policies, MFA, backup procedures and incident reporting. More and more organizations expect this as a minimum standard.
3. Record agreements and make them demonstrable. Agreements on digital security help manage risks and meet NIS2 expectations. Demonstrability via NIS2-QM has now become a decision factor for many customers.

metadata.source_title: More supply chain risks for SMEs

text: Take cyber measures yourself and get started with certification? Then take a look at [Cyber ​​Fundament](https://doorpassen.abnamro.nl/producten-en-diensten/cyber-voor-het-mkb/), especially for SME entrepreneurs, and also ask about the options for NIS2 certification.

text: This article was produced in collaboration with [Samen Digitaal Veilig](https://www.samendigitaalveilig.nl/).

metadata.source_title: More supply chain risks for SMEs

text:
Take cyber measures yourself or report fraud? About 1 in 5 entrepreneurs is confronted with cybercrime. Such a cyber incident can have major consequences for business operations, customers, suppliers or reputation. Taking appropriate measures is therefore important for every company. Do you have an SME company and do you want to reduce your cyber risks?
